---
title: "Introduction to the Finite Element Method"
subtitle: "Lecture 1: Introduction + 1D Setup"
format: 
    revealjs:
        slide-number: true
        multiplex: true
        controls: true
        # incremental: true
        # center: true
        # transition: slide
        margin: 0.1
        css: fem.css
execute:
  echo: true # Show code by default (we'll override for folding)
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

## Setup

```{python}
#| include: false # Usually hide the setup chunk itself

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import sympy as sp
from IPython.display import Math, display

import L1 as fem

# symbolic_fem_workbench imports for live derivations
import sys, os; sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath(".."))
from symbolic_fem_workbench import (
    make_field_1d, weighted_residual, integrate_divergence_1d,
    drop_dirichlet_boundary, apply_neumann_flux, split_linear_weak_form,
    build_bar_1d_local_problem,
)
```

## The strong form {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
fem.display_strong_form()
```

## Derive the weak form of the equation {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
weak_eq = fem.derive_weak_form()
```

## Define shape functions for discretization with N elements {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
N=5
phi, a, b, y_approx, v_approx = fem.define_shape_functions(N)
```

## Assemble the FEM system using the weak form and shape functions {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
K, F_vec = fem.assemble_fem_system(weak_eq, phi, a, b, y_approx, v_approx, N)
```

## All together now! {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
fem.finite_element_method_1d_demo()
```

## Symbolic Workbench: Weak Form Derivation {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
from IPython.display import Math
import sympy as sp
from symbolic_fem_workbench import (
    make_field_1d, weighted_residual, integrate_divergence_1d,
    drop_dirichlet_boundary, apply_neumann_flux, split_linear_weak_form,
)

x, L = sp.symbols("x L", positive=True)
E, A_ = sp.symbols("E A", positive=True)
q, P = sp.symbols("q P", real=True)
u = make_field_1d("u", x)
v = make_field_1d("v", x)
domain = (x, 0, L)

flux = E * A_ * sp.diff(u, x)
domain_term, (left_bc, right_bc) = integrate_divergence_1d(flux, v, x, domain)

print("After integration by parts:")
display(Math(r"\text{Domain: }" + sp.latex(domain_term.as_integral())))
display(Math(r"\text{Left BC: }" + sp.latex(left_bc.evaluate())))
display(Math(r"\text{Right BC: }" + sp.latex(right_bc.evaluate())))

left_val = drop_dirichlet_boundary(left_bc, v)
right_val = apply_neumann_flux(right_bc, flux_expr=flux, prescribed_flux=P)

weak_expr = domain_term.as_integral() - sp.Integral(q * v, domain) + left_val + right_val
wf = split_linear_weak_form(weak_expr, u, v)

print("\nBilinear form a(u,v):")
display(Math(sp.latex(wf.bilinear)))
print("Linear form F(v):")
display(Math(sp.latex(wf.linear)))
```

## Symbolic Workbench: Element Stiffness {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: fenced
from symbolic_fem_workbench import build_bar_1d_local_problem

problem = build_bar_1d_local_problem()
print("Element stiffness matrix Ke:")
display(Math(r"K_e = " + sp.latex(problem["Ke"])))
print("Element load vector fe:")
display(Math(r"f_e = " + sp.latex(problem["fe"])))
```

## Example problem: {auto-animate="true" .smaller .scrollable}
```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| echo: true
from ipywidgets import interact, IntSlider
@interact(n_elements=IntSlider(min=1, max=50, step=1, value=2, description='# Elements:'))
def interactive_solver_plot(n_elements):
    # This function is called whenever the slider changes
    # It calls your original solver/plotter function
    fig = fem.solve_specific_1d_problem_animated(n_elements)
    # ipywidgets automatically displays the returned figure
    return fig

```
